name: Amplitude API
what_it_does: Behavioral analytics platform for tracking user events and deriving product insights, with HTTP API for event ingestion and Dashboard REST API for querying analytics data programmatically.
use_cases: Sending user behavior events from agent-driven workflows
Querying funnel, retention, and segmentation data via Dashboard REST API
Exporting raw event data for warehouse ingestion
Identifying and updating user profiles with custom properties
Fetching cohort definitions for downstream targeting
not_for: Real-time streaming analytics at millisecond latency
Small teams without product analytics needs
best_when: You need to track product behavior events and query behavioral analytics data from agents with good SQL-like querying capabilities.
avoid_when: You need real-time streaming analytics or primarily track server/infrastructure metrics.

auth.notes: HTTP API uses API key + secret key pair. Dashboard REST API uses HTTP Basic auth with API key:secret. Simple key-based auth throughout.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Starter: unlimited events, 2 years data retention (limited features)
pricing.paid_tiers: Plus from $49/month, Growth and Enterprise custom pricing
pricing.notes: Starter plan is genuinely useful for event ingestion. Dashboard REST API queries require Plus or higher. Pricing based on monthly tracked users (MTU).

performance.rate_limits: HTTP API: 30 events/second per device; batch upload recommended for agents

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: HTTP API returns numeric response codes in body. Dashboard REST API has better error detail.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Insert_id field enables deduplication within 7-day window. Batch upload is not inherently idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Batch upload API has a 20MB payload limit - agents must chunk large uploads
Dashboard REST API queries are rate-limited and can time out on complex queries
MTU pricing model: every unique user ID tracked counts toward monthly limit
Data availability delay: events typically available for querying after a few minutes
API and secret key should not be confused - both required for Dashboard REST API

security_score_components.security_notes: API key + secret key for HTTP API. Project-level keys without granular scopes. SOC2 Type II, GDPR. Privacy-first features like data masking and user deletion API for CCPA/GDPR compliance.
